title: Bounded Host Toolchain Availability Decision
objective: Consume the Packet 002 closeout evidence, classify the no-install host toolchain blockers precisely, and choose the single next bounded packet needed before Milestone 2 toolchain and validation readiness can be claimed from the Olares host.
active_role: Olares Developer Residency Toolchain Decision Operator
dependency_note: Packet 001 closed affirmative and established developer-host cutover as the controlling prerequisite before resumed product delivery. Packet 002 then closed with a split verdict: Milestone 1 host residency baseline passed from `/home/olares/code/apex`, but minimum host-side validation for `apps/operations-web` and `packages/calc-engine` is blocked under no-install rules because `pnpm`, root and operations-web `node_modules`, and `pytest` are missing on the host. Packet 003 is the bounded decision surface that must choose the next truthful toolchain path without authorizing installs or delivery work by default.
required_inputs: Packet 002 host path authority evidence from `/home/olares/code/apex` and `/home/olares/code/apex/apex-power-ops-platform`
Packet 002 old-clone observation proving `/home/olares/src/apex-power-ops-platform` remained observe-only
Packet 002 host-local boundary evidence for `~/apex-secrets`, `~/apex-data`, and `~/apex-backups`
Packet 002 toolchain observations showing Node and Python present while `pnpm`, `pytest`, and host `node_modules` are absent under no-install rules

The still-closed no-go boundary around installs, package or lockfile mutation, runtime or service mutation, feature delivery, public ingress change, AI-services expansion, Gitea transition, canonical-hosting transition, old-clone mutation, and remote rewrite
constrained_outputs: A dated decision handoff stating the exact blocker classes preventing Milestone 2 host-side validation readiness
A single next bounded packet recommendation for the toolchain path forward
An explicit statement of what remains closed while the toolchain decision is pending
No installs, no package or lockfile mutation, no runtime or service mutation, no feature delivery, no old-clone mutation, and no host publication claim beyond the Packet 002 evidence
validation_steps: Re-read Packet 002 evidence and confirm the host residency baseline result remains `pass` while the blocked commands and missing no-install tooling are exactly stated.
Classify the blocker surface precisely: command-path absence, missing dependency trees, missing Python test tooling, or another no-install constraint materially evidenced by Packet 002.

Decide the single next bounded packet: existing host toolchain path revalidation if hidden tooling is plausibly present, explicit install-authority decision if new tooling would be required, admissible alternate validation target decision if Milestone 2 can close without those exact surfaces, or defer if none is truthful under current closures.

Keep the result decision-only. Do not install, download, activate, mutate packages, mutate lockfiles, mutate runtimes, mutate services, mutate the old clone, publish feature claims, or reopen product delivery.
Update the roadmap or routing only as needed so the next live packet is unambiguous.

decision_branches.meaning: Choose this only if existing host-local toolchain surfaces appear present but undiscovered or not on PATH under the no-install boundary.
Choose this if Milestone 2 cannot proceed truthfully without a later separately packetized install or materialization decision.
Choose this only if a narrower already-admitted host validation slice can satisfy the milestone intent without pnpm or pytest installation.
Choose this if no truthful near-term validation move exists under the current no-install and no-mutation boundary.

decision_rationale: Packet 002 did not merely find undiscovered PATH ambiguity; it proved `pnpm` is missing from PATH, root `node_modules` is absent, `apps/operations-web/node_modules` is absent, and Python test tooling is missing.

The default application validation target `apps/operations-web` cannot truthfully validate under no-install rules without a usable pnpm/dependency tree or equivalent pre-existing TypeScript toolchain.
The default shared package target `packages/calc-engine` cannot truthfully validate under no-install rules without pytest or an explicitly accepted alternate validation command.

Choosing an alternate target would not satisfy the Packet 002 blocker truth because the active lane still needs a real host toolchain materialization decision before Milestone 2 readiness can be claimed.
Therefore the smallest truthful next packet is a decision-only toolchain materialization authority gate, not an install, retry, feature-delivery packet, or validation-readiness claim.

blocker_classification.evidence: `pnpm` missing from PATH during Packet 002 host validation
root `node_modules` and `apps/operations-web/node_modules` absent on the host
`python3 -m pytest` failed with `No module named pytest`

next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 004 - Bounded Host Toolchain Materialization Authority Decision
sql_permission_note: Decision only. This packet authorizes no database writes, installs, package mutation, lockfile mutation, runtime mutation, service mutation, public ingress change, feature delivery, old-clone mutation, or remote rewrite.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-05 as a decision-only packet. Packet 003 selects Branch B: a later explicit host toolchain materialization authority decision is required before any install, dependency materialization, host-side validation readiness claim, or resumed product delivery. Packet 003 itself authorizes no installs, package or lockfile mutation, runtime/service mutation, feature delivery, public ingress, code-hosting transition, old-clone mutation, remote rewrite, rollback, force, reset, or clean.
next_action: Execute `Olares Dev Residency 004 - Bounded Host Toolchain Materialization Authority Decision` as a separate decision-only packet before any toolchain materialization or Milestone 2 validation readiness claim.
